text.title: arch-pattern-onion Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for arch-pattern-onion as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Putting domain service logic inside Application Services ("fat application service" that does cross-entity calculations).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cross-entity domain logic belongs in Domain Services, not Application Services. Application Services should orchestrate; Domain Services should compute. Mixing them defeats testability and conceptual clarity.

failure-mode.description: Domain entities depending on EF Core DbContext or any ORM concept.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The Domain layer must have zero dependencies. EF Core navigation properties and data annotations are infrastructure concerns. Use separate persistence models and map them in the Infrastructure layer.

failure-mode.description: Using ORM entities as domain entities (same class serves both purposes).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: ORM schemas change on database needs; domain entities change on business rules. Conflating them creates a permanent coupling that makes both harder to evolve independently.

failure-mode.description: Having circular dependencies between Domain and Application layers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The dependency rule is strict: Application → Domain, not the other way. If Domain needs something from Application, extract it to Domain or invert the dependency via an interface.
